behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies because correctness depends on real speech-to-text model diarization output (an AI/ML judgment), not just local parsing. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed STT response with perfect labels would always pass and hide real failures where the provider drops, renames, or misaligns speaker tags. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit would miss real diarization errors from actual audio; Integration with mocked STT would miss provider-specific label formats/instability. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: behavior text targets one agentic boundary (STT label preservation), not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker recording through the production STT API with diarization enabled, then assert returned transcript contains stable per-utterance speaker labels that can be mapped to attendee identities for downstream summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS does not force escalation: this behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic and can be validated at one local function/object boundary with no I/O. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP do not apply. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: behavior text is about extraction logic itself, not a required cross-module handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignments, assert parser output includes normalized records with both assignee and task text, and excludes non-assignment statements.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output content quality (coverage of assigned attendees), so LLM-DEP applies. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic mocked summaries can satisfy coverage regardless of real model omissions. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses prompt/output-level omission behavior entirely; Integration with stubbed LLM misses stochastic under-coverage where some assignees are dropped. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: behavior can be validated with a single real summarization decision from transcript-to-summary, not necessarily multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a transcript with known attendee-task assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item across repeated runs within an acceptable pass-rate threshold.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: extractor output must be correctly consumed by schema assembler. MOCK-MISS applies at local-module interaction level, not OS/network/model quality. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit tests of extractor and formatter separately can pass while integration fails due to field-name/shape mismatch that routes decisions to narrative. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; (b) behavior says "identified decisions" (already identified), so no dependence on model judgment; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration requirement. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic/Workflow is disqualified by behavior text focusing on routing of already identified items.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real decision-extraction and summary-assembly modules in-process; provide transcript/input fixtures with pre-identifiable decisions and assert resulting structured object populates only `decisions` schema fields, not narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model output quality and placement semantics in live summary generation, so LLM-DEP applies. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed model outputs can be forced to place decisions correctly and hide real misses/misplacement. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses generation behavior; Integration with deterministic fixtures misses real-model variation where decisions are paraphrased into narrative instead of `decisions`. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: behavior can be exercised with a single transcript->summary model decision; multiple agentic stages are not explicitly required.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known decision statements, call the production summarization model, and assert those decisions are represented in the generated `decisions` section (not only narrative text), measured across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP applies: behavior explicitly requires sequential segment processing and merge across repeated model calls. It is not just one agentic decision; correctness depends on chained agentic outputs and handoff state. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses cross-segment ordering/state bugs; Integration with stubs misses coherence failures from real per-segment model variance; Agentic single-step tests miss errors emerging only across multi-call sequencing/merge (duplicate, contradictory, or dropped items).
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation, per-segment transcription/summarization, and merge path; assert final summary is singular, schema-valid, and preserves cross-segment action items/decisions without contradiction or duplication beyond tolerance.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end orchestration over multiple stages, including real model calls, so both REAL-INFRA and MULTI-STEP agentic dependencies are in scope; minimum boundary is Workflow. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit misses inter-stage failures entirely; Integration misses real infra/auth/timeout/backpressure and provider quirks; Agentic single-step misses cross-stage automation failures (upload->transcribe->summarize->delivery handoff/retry dead-ends). REJECTION-GATE: no concrete behavior text disqualifies higher level; behavior explicitly names full pipeline with real recording.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end run with a real meeting file: upload, STT call, LLM summarization, and downstream delivery sink; assert completion SLA, no human retries, and valid structured output containing required sections.
